test_description: For each masked temperature map, transform to spherical harmonics on the masked sky, retain only multipoles 2 <= l <= 30, and synthesize the corresponding low-l filtered map at nside=256. Split the unmasked pixels into fixed ecliptic north and south hemispheres. In each hemisphere, subtract that hemisphere’s unmasked-pixel mean and compute the pixel-area-weighted variance, V_N and V_S. The anomaly statistic is the single scalar A = ln(V_S / V_N), so positive values indicate excess low-l variance in the southern ecliptic hemisphere relative to the northern one. Compare the Planck SMICA value of A to the empirical distribution from the 1000 identically processed LCDM simulations.
test_hypothesis: Planck has an anomalously large positive low-l ecliptic variance log-contrast, A = ln(V_S/V_N), meaning excess low-l variance in the southern ecliptic hemisphere relative to the northern hemisphere compared with LCDM simulations.
justificaton: The statistic is explicitly oriented so that larger values indicate stronger southern ecliptic variance excess, and the code identifies the “tail_of_interest” as the upper tail for excess southern low-ell variance. Therefore the anomaly being tested has a clear directional preference toward large positive A.

custom_summary.test_description: Fixed Ecliptic Low-l Variance Log-Contrast: transform each masked temperature map to masked-sky pseudo-spherical-harmonic coefficients, keep only 2 <= ell <= 30, synthesize the low-ell map at nside=256, and return A = ln(V_S/V_N), the ecliptic south-to-north hemisphere variance log-contrast.
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: upper tail for excess low-ell variance in the southern ecliptic hemisphere; lower tail indicates northern excess
custom_summary.ecliptic_coordinate_assumption: Input HEALPix maps are in Galactic coordinates; the fixed ecliptic hemispheres use the J2000 ecliptic north pole at Galactic (l,b)=(96.384,29.812) deg.

plot_spec.title: Fixed ecliptic low-ell variance log-contrast
plot_spec.caption: Distribution of the ell=2..30 masked-sky filtered ecliptic south-to-north variance log-contrast. Positive values mean larger low-ell variance in the southern ecliptic hemisphere.

custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is in the highest 5% upper tail: enhanced southern ecliptic low-ell variance relative to simulations.

test_summary.Description: Masked SMICA and 1000 LCDM simulations were filtered to 2 <= l <= 30, split into fixed ecliptic north/south hemispheres, demeaned per hemisphere, and compared using A = ln(V_S/V_N).
test_summary.Results: Planck gives A = 0.480, i.e. V_S/V_N = 1.62. Simulations have mean A = 0.004 and std = 0.209. Planck is at the 98.3rd percentile, with 17/1000 simulations larger: one-tailed upper p = 0.017, about 2.12 sigma.
test_summary.Interpretation: The statistic shows a significant positive southern ecliptic low-l variance excess relative to LCDM simulations, passing the 5% one-sided threshold but not the 1% threshold.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: No directly matching published test of this fixed ecliptic low-l variance log-contrast was found in the provided/previous searches. It is broadly related to CMB hemispherical power-asymmetry and local-variance studies, but uses a specific fixed ecliptic split and log variance ratio.
